_comment_top: cargoless SigNoz/Grafana-import dashboard sketch. Authored 2026-05-20 against origin/main=929a5d3. PURPOSE: importable+readable starting point for the operator-facing dashboard once Wave-2 (Increment-5 5d) metrics land. Some panels query Wave-1-landed surfaces (traces/keystone-spans/events) and will render today; others query Wave-2-future metrics and will render once those metrics emit. EACH PANEL'S '_status' FIELD indicates which. Companion design doc: docs/design/D-INC2-OBSERVABILITY.md. Companion runbook: docs/observability/AC4-DIVERGENCE-RUNBOOK.md.
_comment_format: Grafana-format JSON (schemaVersion 39) for SigNoz import — matches the tf-multiverse/deployment/monitoring/dashboards/ convention. SigNoz's UI can import this directly; per-panel queries use Prometheus-compatible PromQL syntax that SigNoz's metric backend understands.
_comment_status_legend: WAVE-1-LIVE = queries Wave-1 keystone span/event data, renders today; WAVE-2-PENDING = queries Wave-2 metric, renders once Wave-2 lands.
title: cargoless — Observability Overview

list.name: cluster_hash
list._comment: Populates from Wave-2 cargoless_ra_resident_bytes gauge labels. Pre-Wave-2: shows no values; All-selector still selects every series.

list.name: Daemon Restart Events
list._comment: WAVE-2-PENDING — annotates daemon restarts on every panel once cargoless_initial_spawn_total emits.

panels.title: Service status — daemon up
panels._status: WAVE-1-LIVE (uses /healthz scrape if Prometheus configured, OR up{} metric from service-discovery)
panels._comment: Status indicator — green if at least one cargoless instance has reported in the last minute. Use SigNoz service health if your collector exposes it; alternatively wire to the /healthz endpoint shipped via #236.

panels.title: RA processes alive
panels._comment: Count of distinct RA processes currently reporting RSS. Model-R thesis says this stays at ≈1 per cluster across N worktrees; if it grows linearly with worktrees, the fleet-RAM multiplication regression has re-emerged.

panels.title: Verdict rate (5m, green/red)
panels._status: WAVE-2-PENDING (cargoless_verdict_total is Wave-2). Pre-Wave-2: a degraded version queries SigNoz trace search for count of verdict.publish span emissions.

panels.title: AC4 Divergence — overlay.reset − (ra.restart + initial.spawn)
panels.description: **LOAD-BEARING INVARIANT.** This timeseries MUST stay flat at 0 forever. Any non-zero value persisting beyond 5 minutes is a regression of the proven-core-precondition-violated-at-integration-seam class — see docs/observability/AC4-DIVERGENCE-RUNBOOK.md. Do NOT silence the alert; do NOT restart the daemon; page dev-fixer for source-fix at the violating seam.
panels._status: WAVE-2-PENDING (all three metrics in expression are Wave-2)
panels._comment: The headline AC4 panel. Per-cluster_hash slicing is the diagnostic drill-down (Panel 21 below). Aggregated view here = fleet-wide divergence. Signed view chosen deliberately per D-INC2-OBSERVABILITY §9 q3 — negative indicates transient ordering effect (resolves within one scrape cycle); persistent non-zero = real regression.

panels.title: AC4 Divergence — per cluster_hash drill-down
panels._comment: Per-cluster_hash divergence. Identifies the specific cluster the AC4 alert is firing on. See runbook §3.2.

panels.title: Save → verdict latency — p50/p95/p99
panels.description: The AC#2 KPI. Wave-1-tier: RA-incremental hint (median ≤1s); Wave-1-tier: authoritative cargo-check verdict (seconds, no sub-1s promise). Tracked by tier label.

panels.title: Verdict counter by color (5m rate)
panels._comment: Green/red transition ratio. A sustained shift toward red indicates either real defect surge or false-RED noise (e.g. flycheck timing).

panels.title: Fleet RAM — Model-R structural claim made VISIBLE
RA process resident bytes — per cluster_hash
panels.description: Visualizes the Model-R fleet-RAM claim: one multiplexed RA across N worktrees; total RSS stays ≈1 GiB regardless of worktree count. Each line = one cluster's RA RSS; the SUM across all lines is the fleet's total. Mechanism check: line count == cluster count, NEVER worktree count.

panels.title: Fleet total RA RSS (sum across clusters)
Active RA count (= cluster count)
panels._comment: Should equal cluster count (Model-R: one RA per cluster_hash). If this number grows with worktree count, the multiplexing regression has re-emerged.

panels.title: Pushed-mode worktrees (post-Inc-2)
panels._comment: Worktrees being served via POST /overlay ingest (no shared FS). Post-#240 Inc-2 surface.

panels.title: HTTP server request p95 by route
Cluster transition rate (spawn / reap / swap)
panels._comment: Cluster lifecycle activity. Compare with the RA-count gauge (Panel 42) — a healthy fleet shows spawn/reap pairs balanced over the long run.

panels.title: Overlay.switch p95 by worktree (Top 10)
panels._comment: Per-worktree overlay-switch latency. Slow worktrees here = candidate for cluster-isolation analysis (a noisy WT can contend with siblings sharing the same RA).

targets.expr: topk(10, histogram_quantile(0.95, sum by (worktree, le) (rate(cargoless_overlay_switch_seconds_bucket{service="$service", worktree=~"$worktree"}[5m]))))

panels.title: Wave-1 keystone span surface (traces, available today)
Wave-1 keystone span emission counts (5m)
panels.description: **WAVE-1-LIVE** — these panels query SigNoz trace search rather than Wave-2 metrics, so they render today. Useful as the pre-Wave-2 substitute for the metric counter panels and as a long-running cross-check after Wave-2 lands (metric_total should == event_count over the same window, modulo sampling).
panels._comment: In SigNoz, replace the Prometheus query with a SigNoz Query Builder query against trace data: name IN ('ra.spawn', 'ra.respawn', 'overlay.reset', 'overlay.switch', 'verdict.publish') AND service.name = $service, group by name, count(). The Grafana-format below is the closest representation; operators editing this in SigNoz UI should switch to the native trace-query view.

_comment_post_wave2_actions: Once Wave-2 lands and metrics emit, every panel marked WAVE-2-PENDING transitions to live data without dashboard edits. The annotations panel (Daemon Restart Events) starts firing at restart-time. The AC4 Divergence panel (20/21) begins enforcing the invariant; pair it with the alert defined in docs/observability/AC4-DIVERGENCE-RUNBOOK.md §1.1.
_comment_alert_export: This dashboard does NOT contain SigNoz alert rules — those are configured in SigNoz's alerts UI per docs/observability/AC4-DIVERGENCE-RUNBOOK.md. Define the alert there pointing at the AC4 divergence expression with FOR=5m and labels severity=critical, signal=stop-class-regression. A future revision of this dashboard JSON could include alert-rule blocks once the SigNoz import format stabilizes for them.
